description: Runtime rubric that exercises the analysis pipeline against five golden spend scenarios and scores the quality of skill outputs on 8 deterministic dimensions. Complements run_eval.py (reference-data quality) and run_feature_eval.py (structural completeness). This eval targets the question: given correct wiring, does the pipeline produce high-quality, faithful, evidence-grounded recommendations?

domains.name: skill_output_integrity
domains.description: Arithmetic correctness, internal consistency, and specificity of what each skill computes.

dimensions.name: Numerical Faithfulness
dimensions.description: Arithmetic self-consistency within skill outputs. Checks: (a) profiler total_spend matches sum-of-categories within 1%; (b) confidence bands are monotonically ordered low≤mid≤high; (c) deduped_mid_savings ≤ gross_3yr for every value_matrix row; (d) net_npv ≤ gross_3yr for every row (NPV is discounted, so strictly ≤ gross).

dimensions.name: Recommendation Specificity
dimensions.description: Each initiative in the value bridge must carry a specific lever name (not empty/unknown), a non-empty root_cause, a positive payback_months, and a recognized confidence level. Savings-modeler initiatives must also include a non-empty assumptions list.

dimensions.name: Evidence Grounding
dimensions.description: Every category appearing in the value bridge must be traceable to at least one benchmark lens (peer, internal, or heuristic). Also checks that peer_benchmarker carries a non-empty source attribution and that each comparison row has an estimated_saving_amount ≥ 0.

dimensions.name: Priority Ranking Correctness
dimensions.description: value_matrix must be sorted descending by deduped_mid_savings. Inversion count (pairwise i<j where savings[i] < savings[j]) determines penalty. A perfectly sorted list scores 10; each inversion reduces the score proportionally.

dimensions.name: Causal Reasoning Quality
dimensions.description: Root-cause-analyzer findings must carry specific diagnoses (≥15 chars, not a generic fallback), a named recommended_lever, and a non-empty implementation_approach. Empty or absent findings when categories are above-benchmark is treated as a coverage gap.
dimensions.scoring_method: quality_findings / total_findings × 10; if no findings and categories above P50, score = 4.0

domains.name: signal_coverage
domains.description: Whether all high-impact signals (benchmarks, variance, compliance, contracts) are surfaced into skill outputs, and whether the platform composes a decision-quality advisory for category-focused asks.

dimensions.name: Coverage Completeness
dimensions.description: All high-spend categories should appear in the value bridge. Conditional signals: if budget data is present, bva_analyzer must fire with bva_available=True and at least one variance; if MSME-flagged lines exist, msme_compliance must report at_risk data; if contract expiry lines are present, contract_lifecycle must report renewal_alerts.

dimensions.name: Decision Memo Quality
dimensions.description: For category-focused scenarios (S03), the focus category must have complete coverage across all four benchmark lenses: value bridge entry, peer comparison, root-cause finding, and heuristic finding. Non-category-focused scenarios receive a neutral 7.5 on this dimension.
dimensions.scoring_method: 4 binary checks × 2.5 pts each = 10 max (only scored on S03; other scenarios get 7.5)

dimensions.name: Action Timeframe Clarity
dimensions.description: Savings-modeler initiatives must have realistic payback timelines (1–36 months), at least one quick-win opportunity (payback ≤ 6 months), and non-zero cost-to-achieve values (zero CTA suggests the lever was not properly costed).
